title: phippsia borealis subsp. neglecta
intro: Phippsia borealis subsp. neglecta is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. It is also known as the "Himalayan Pearlwort" and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting potential antiviral activity. The herbal preparations of Phippsia borealis subsp. neglecta include infusion, decoction, and tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
